channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: DRDO Developing 800 km BrahMos-ER and Submarine-Launched Variant for India’s Project-77 Nuclear Attack Submarines
item.description: India’s Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) has confirmed that work is underway on an extended-range version of the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile with a range of around 800 kilometers. The new variant, known as BrahMos-ER, is being developed primarily for the Indian Army and Indian Air Force, allowing both services to conduct long-range precision strikes against high-value targets at significantly greater stand-off distances.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s PFBR and the Strategic Plutonium Equation: How Fast Breeder Reactors Could Expand Nuclear Deterrence Capacity
item.description: India’s Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam is primarily framed as a cornerstone of the country’s long-term energy security strategy, but its implications extend into the strategic domain in ways that are increasingly being discussed within defense and nuclear policy circles. While the official mandate of the PFBR is civilian—focused on closing the nuclear fuel cycle and maximizing energy extraction from uranium and thorium—its operational characteristics introduce a latent capability that could significantly alter India’s fissile material balance if ever required.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IG Defence Developing Turbojet-Powered Loitering Munition with 100 km Range for High-Speed Precision Strikes
item.description: India’s emerging defence startup ecosystem is rapidly moving into advanced strike technologies, with IG Defence confirming the development of its IG Turbojet Loitering Munition, a high-performance system designed for long-range, high-speed precision engagements. The announcement comes alongside the company’s ongoing work on the 1000 km-class KAL unmanned strike platform, indicating a broader roadmap focused on indigenous autonomous strike capabilities.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Tejas Mk2’s 120-Minute Combat Endurance with 8 BVR Missiles Marks a Quantum Leap Over Tejas Mk1A
item.description: The Tejas Mk2 is poised to significantly enhance the Indian Air Force’s air defence and offensive capabilities by delivering nearly double the combat patrol endurance of its predecessor while carrying a much heavier beyond-visual-range missile loadout. Senior officials from the Aeronautical Development Agency have confirmed that the Tejas Mk2 will achieve approximately 120 minutes of mission endurance in a fighting profile while armed with eight BVR missiles. In comparison, the Tejas Mk1 and Mk1A variants offer only around 57 minutes of endurance when carrying four BVR missiles under similar conditions.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: From MIRVs to Bunker Busters: India’s Shift Toward Flexible Nuclear and Conventional Deterrence
item.description: India’s strategic deterrence posture is evolving in response to a rapidly changing security environment. While the principle of credible minimum deterrence remains officially unchanged, the means of achieving it are becoming increasingly sophisticated and flexible. One of the most significant developments in this regard is the adaptation of long-range ballistic missiles, such as Agni-V, for conventional roles.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Himalayan Defense Strategy: Lessons from Iran’s Underground ‘Missile Cities’ and the Path to Robust Deterrence
item.description: India faces persistent challenges along its northern frontier with China, where the rugged Himalayas serve as both a natural barrier and a contested zone. The suggestion that Indian Armed Forces draw inspiration from Iran’s strategy of constructing “war cities” or underground missile complexes buried deep in mountainous terrain has gained attention in strategic discussions. Iran has invested decades in developing extensive networks of hardened underground facilities, often referred to as missile cities, excavated hundreds of meters into granite and limestone formations to protect ballistic missiles, launch systems, and support infrastructure from aerial strikes.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF-Led Joint Tender for 7 Surveillance UAVs Enters Technical Evaluation Phase
item.description: India’s latest push to strengthen its surveillance capabilities has entered a crucial phase as the tender for seven high-endurance Surveillance Drones/UAVs (V2 category) has officially moved into the Technical Evaluation Phase following the closure of bid submissions on March 30, 2026. The procurement, being led by the Indian Air Force on behalf of all three services, reflects a growing emphasis on integrated, tri-service drone capabilities tailored for diverse operational environments.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Navy To Hold Commanders’ Conference From April 14, Review Strategy As Energy Security Takes Centre Stage
item.description: The Indian Navy will hold the inaugural biannual Commanders’ Conference from April 14 to 16, at Nausena Bhawan in New Delhi, as announced by the Ministry of Defence on Sunday. This Conference takes place amidst the Indian Navy’s deployment in the Indian Ocean, where it is tasked with protecting oil tankers that are travelling towards India after passing through the Strait of Hormuz, in light of the ongoing conflict in West Asia.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Jaishankar meets UAE Deputy PM, discusses regional situation
item.description: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar met UAE Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Abdullah bin Zayed Al Nahyan in Abu Dhabi and discussed the evolving regional situation and its implications. Jaishankar is in Abu Dhabi in the concluding leg of his four-day two-nation visit to Mauritius and the UAE from April 9-12.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Bad news’: Vance says no agreement after Iran peace talks in Pakistan
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance said on Sunday that his negotiating team was leaving Pakistan after not reaching a deal with Iran after 21 hours of negotiations.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Govt permits 4 Iranian oil tankers to berth for Reliance at Sikka port
item.description: India’s shipping ministry has granted special permission to four vessels carrying Iranian oil – as requested by Reliance Industries – to berth at the western port of Sikka, three industry sources said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Turns Crisis Into Opportunity By Augmenting Strategic Petroleum Reserve Capacities, Diversifying Imports
item.description: The ongoing crisis in the Middle East has created opportunities for India in various ways, ranging from augmenting strategic petroleum reserve capacities at home to diversifying imports, a report highlighted on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: MEA says deeply concerned by reports of civilian casualties in Lebanon
item.description: India is “deeply concerned” by reports of civilian casualties in Lebanon, the Indian foreign ministry said on Friday, two days after Israel’s worst bombardment in its war with Lebanon killed more than 300 people.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Iran Wanted US V-P Vance To Join Talks; ‘Pak’s Role Choreographed’: Former Envoy Veena Sikri
item.description: Former High Commissioner of India to Bangladesh, Veena Sikri, said on Saturday that Iran had urged the US Vice-President (V-P) J.D. Vance to join the peace talks currently underway in Pakistan’s Islamabad, because of his anti-war approach. She also called Pakistan’s role in facilitation of the talks as “choreographed” by the US.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Search Ops continues in Manipur after BSF constable’s killing; 21 illegal bunkers destroyed
item.description: Security forces on Sunday continued their joint combing operations for the third consecutive day on Sunday to nab militants following the killing of a BSF constable in Manipur’s Ukhrul district. During the ongoing operations, forces also destroyed 21 illegally constructed bunkers in the district, officials said.
item.dc:creator: admin
